assertions.title: Mission-invoking E2E flow runs in Mission-GUI-TEST using Custom CCH-GPT-5.4

steps.action: Connect agent-browser to the already-running isolated Electron app on CDP port 9222 and inspect the active Mission surface.
steps.expected: A Mission-GUI-TEST Mission session is visible on the Electron Mission surface.
steps.observed: Connected successfully to Electron 40 on 127.0.0.1:9222; the live UI showed project `Mission-GUI-TEST`, active Mission DOM markers (`mission-page`, `mission-statusbar`, `mission-view-toggle`), and sidebar Mission rows including `session-mission-f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc`.

steps.action: Read the active session body text and combobox text from the live Electron surface.
steps.expected: The constrained proof session remains on Custom -> CCH-GPT-5.4 without any OS-level project picker step.
steps.observed: The active Mission session body showed `Mission-GUI-TEST`, message text for the live kill-worker proof session, and the model chip text `CCH-GPT-5.4`; no native project picker or Web/LAN surface appeared during the check.

steps.action: Cross-check the persisted isolated session JSON for the active proof session.
steps.expected: The saved Mission session points at the Mission-GUI-TEST project and a Custom CCH GPT model.
steps.observed: `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc.json` records `projectDir`, `workspaceDir`, and `repoRoot` under `/Users/clive/Documents/Mission-GUI-TEST` and stores `model: "custom:CCH-Code-GPT-5.4-2"` with explicit Mission metadata.

evidence.consoleErrors: none after `agent-browser errors --clear`; the subsequent `agent-browser errors` check returned no output.
evidence.network: No live renderer requests were captured during the read-only check. Supporting transport/state evidence came from `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc.json`, `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/validation/mission-foundation/user-testing/flows/foundation-routing.json`, and `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/validation/mission-verification/user-testing/flows/kill-worker-pause-proof.json`.

assertions.title: Create → propose → accept → run → validator injection → validation completion works end to end

steps.action: Review the checked-in feature-owned cross-flow proof bundle for VAL-CROSS-002.
steps.expected: A feature-owned artifact should map the end-to-end lifecycle to concrete checked-in evidence.
steps.observed: `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/validation/mission-verification/feature-owned-proof-followup.json` explicitly marks `VAL-CROSS-002` as `pass` and ties create/propose/run evidence to Mission-GUI-TEST user-surface artifacts plus focused Mission Control and reconciliation tests.

steps.action: Cross-check the live mission directory lifecycle files for validator injection and validation progression.
steps.expected: The mission log should show implementation worker completion followed by milestone validation triggering and validator worker activity in the same mission context.
steps.observed: `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/progress_log.jsonl` contains `worker_completed` entries followed by `milestone_validation_triggered` and later validator worker activity, while `state.json` still tracks the same mission as `running` under `user-testing-validator-mission-verification`.

steps.action: Inspect live Mission UI state for continuity with validator-phase Mission context.
steps.expected: The Electron Mission surface should still show the same Mission shell/state rather than a recreated non-Mission session.
steps.observed: The live Electron Mission surface remained on `mission-page` with Mission status bar and Mission-specific controls visible, matching the validator-phase continuity described by the proof bundle.

evidence.consoleErrors: none during the live corroborating UI check.
evidence.network: Lifecycle evidence came from `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/progress_log.jsonl`, `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/state.json`, `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/validation-state.json`, and the checked-in proof mapping in `feature-owned-proof-followup.json`; no new live network traffic was required for this read-only validation pass.

assertions.title: Restart and reselection recover an existing Mission via Mission entry points

steps.action: Use the real sidebar session entry point to reselect another existing Mission session in Mission-GUI-TEST.
steps.expected: Selecting a Mission sidebar row should keep the app on the Mission surface for that session.
steps.observed: Clicking `session-mission-bf25232d-deba-422a-84ee-b67f0d123e18` switched the active content to `Run the deterministic kill-worker valida...`; DOM inspection still showed `mission-page: true` and the active Mission row changed to that session.

steps.action: Use real project entry points to leave and return to Mission-GUI-TEST.
steps.expected: Project reselection should rebuild a Mission session on the Mission surface instead of dropping to a normal chat route.
steps.observed: After clicking `project-droi` and then `project-Mission-GUI-TEST`, the Electron UI returned to the Mission surface with the Mission proof content and `CCH-GPT-5.4` still visible in the session body.

steps.action: Cross-check checked-in restart/latest-session restore proof artifacts.
steps.expected: Earlier live restart proof should show latest-session restore and reload recovery back into Mission without fresh live notifications.
steps.observed: `foundation-routing.json` records that the live app had already restored an existing Mission-GUI-TEST session on `/mission` before manual interaction, and `restore-bootstrap.json` records `[data-testid="mission-page"]` immediately after reload with `droid.load_session`-based restore evidence.

evidence.network: Read-only reselection produced no tracked live renderer requests. Route/recovery evidence came from the live DOM checks plus `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/validation/mission-foundation/user-testing/flows/foundation-routing.json`, `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/validation/mission-sync-recovery/user-testing/flows/restore-bootstrap.json`, and `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc.json`.

assertions.title: Mission E2E validation stays on the Electron surface with an isolated copied data directory

steps.action: Inspect the Mission testing guidance and services manifest.
steps.expected: The isolated Mission validation surface should be Electron-only and use `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e`, API port 3002, and CDP port 9222 instead of LAN/Web mode.
steps.observed: `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/library/user-testing.md` and `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/services.yaml` both specify Electron-only Mission validation with `DROID_APP_DATA_DIR=/tmp/droi-mission-e2e`, `DROID_APP_API_PORT=3002`, and `ELECTRON_REMOTE_DEBUGGING_PORT=9222`, and explicitly forbid using port 3001/Web-LAN mode.

steps.action: Verify the running isolated Electron instance through the local CDP endpoint.
steps.expected: The validation app should answer on 127.0.0.1:9222 as Electron/Chrome rather than a LAN web deployment.
steps.observed: `curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/version` returned Electron 40 / Chrome 144 metadata for the running Droi desktop app.

steps.action: Capture the live test surface screenshot.
steps.expected: The proof should show the Electron Mission UI itself, not a browser-based LAN surface.
steps.observed: `cross-entry-current-mission.png` shows the desktop Mission shell with `Mission-GUI-TEST`, Mission session rows, and Mission controls; no Web/LAN address flow or OS-native picker was used.

evidence.consoleErrors: none during the live Electron-only inspection.
evidence.network: Terminal/config evidence: `electron-dev.start` in `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/services.yaml` launches `DROID_APP_DATA_DIR=/tmp/droi-mission-e2e DROID_APP_API_PORT=3002 ELECTRON_REMOTE_DEBUGGING_PORT=9222 pnpm dev:test`; the live CDP probe on `http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/version` succeeded; agent-browser captured no requests against `127.0.0.1:3001`.

frictions.description: The older Mission session used for sidebar reselection (`session-mission-bf25232d-deba-422a-84ee-b67f0d123e18`) rendered `Kimi K2.5`, so it could prove Mission entry-point recovery but not the required Custom `CCH-GPT-5.4` constraint on its own.
frictions.resolution: Used the active constrained proof session `f1061069-8673-41ad-a30e-26eafb2e80dc` plus its persisted isolated session JSON for VAL-CROSS-001 model proof, and used the older session only for read-only reselection evidence in VAL-CROSS-006.

frictions.description: After returning via the `project-Mission-GUI-TEST` button, the sidebar session list collapsed so the active `data-active` Mission row was no longer exposed even though Mission content stayed visible.
frictions.resolution: Confirmed recovery via the still-visible Mission page/body content, the `CCH-GPT-5.4` chip, and the supporting restart/reselection artifacts from `foundation-routing.json` and `restore-bootstrap.json`.

summary: Validated 4 assigned cross-entry/recovery assertions: VAL-CROSS-001 pass, VAL-CROSS-002 pass, VAL-CROSS-006 pass, VAL-CROSS-007 pass. Fresh live Electron evidence came from the isolated app on CDP 9222 plus screenshots `cross-entry-current-mission.png`, `cross-entry-reselected-other-mission.png`, and `cross-entry-project-return.png`; lifecycle-heavy assertions were corroborated with the checked-in feature-owned proof bundle, foundation/restore flow artifacts, and the live mission files under `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda`.
